title: A High-Dimensional Statistical Method for Optimizing Transfer Quantities in Multi-Source Transfer Learning

repos.name: zqy0126__OTQMS
repos.evidence: Repo owner 'zqy0126' matches first author 'Qingyue Zhang'. README commands run 'sources_model_pretrain' and 'main' with '--otherpoint_list ["m","all","none"]' where 'm' is OTQMS. Paper states code at https://github.com/zqy0126/OTQMS. Repo contains misc.py:fisher_and_get_multis_optimal_m, solve_quadratic_problem (the core optimal-quantity selection), algorithms.py (Computheta), datasets.py (DomainNet/OfficeHome), and output_images/ with PDFs matching paper figures (OTQMS_domain_selection, teaser_on_Cifar10, data_usage_runtime_comparison). This is the paper's own released implementation.

rationale: One repo was cloned: zqy0126__OTQMS. The paper explicitly states code at https://github.com/zqy0126/OTQMS. The repo owner matches the first author (Qingyue Zhang = zqy0126), the repo name OTQMS is the paper's algorithm name, the README runs the exact commands described in Algorithm 1, and the code implements the paper's core components (Fisher-based optimal transfer quantity selection, OTQMS training loop, DomainNet/OfficeHome evaluation). The audit correctly identified this as the author code and wrote substantive findings against it (objective-extra-term, fisher-minmax-normalization, pretrained-ckpt-path-mismatch, lr-mismatch, model-selection-on-test, etc.), all of which are legitimate code-vs-paper discrepancies within the core implementation. No non-core repos were cloned.
audit_impact: None. The cloned repo is correctly identified as the paper's core implementation. All findings are written against the right code. The audit verdict (source present) is correct.
recommended_action: No change needed. The provenance is clean: one core repo, correctly classified, with all findings targeted at genuine paper-vs-code discrepancies.
